meta_title: For Students: marketing automation to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Marketing Automation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to build a marketing automation tool to overcome project paralysis. A comprehensive case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for inspiration. Build a marketing automation tool that monitors social trends and drafts content. This project teaches you API orchestration, prompt engineering, and database management—all while solving the 'no project ideas' dilemma. Use Antigravity to scaffold your backend and Claude Code to iterate your logic quickly.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking in a rhythmic taunt. You have the technical skills, but the portfolio remains empty because every idea feels either too trivial or impossibly complex. It is the classic 'student trap' of analysis paralysis, where the fear of building the wrong thing prevents you from building anything at all.

But the secret to a world-class portfolio isn't a revolutionary idea; it's the execution of a functional utility. By building a marketing automation tool, you transform from a student into a builder who understands how to connect data, logic, and delivery. Let’s break the cycle and build something that actually moves the needle.

steps.title: Define the Automation Scope
steps.description: Narrow your focus to a specific pain point, such as auto-generating LinkedIn summaries from RSS feeds. This limits scope creep while ensuring a finished product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a project architecture for a Node.js marketing automation tool that fetches RSS feeds, summarizes them via LLM, and stores them in a Supabase instance.

steps.title: Establish Data Pipelines
steps.description: Build the ingestion layer. You need a robust way to fetch external data without hitting rate limits or crashing your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a resilient RSS feed scraper using axios and cheerio with built-in error handling and exponential backoff retry logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-feature 'RSS scraper with rate limiting'

steps.title: Integrate LLM Content Processing
steps.description: Connect your pipeline to an LLM to turn raw data into actionable marketing content. This is where the 'automation' magic happens.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a service that takes raw text input and outputs a professional marketing post optimized for LinkedIn, including emojis and hashtags.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create content generation service using OpenAI API'

steps.title: Implement Database Persistence
steps.description: Ensure your content is saved. A portfolio project without a database is just a script; with a database, it's a SaaS application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for storing scraped articles, generated posts, and their publication status using Prisma.

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Deploy your application so you can demo it. A live URL is worth a thousand lines of code on GitHub.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Dockerfile and a GitHub Actions workflow to deploy this application to a cloud provider on every push to main.

pro_tips: Focus on 'Read-to-Write' ratios: Spend 20% of your time reading documentation and 80% shipping code to avoid tutorial hell.
Use environment variables for all API keys; leaking a key is the fastest way to kill a project's credibility.
Build a 'dry run' mode: Always add a flag to simulate actions without posting to social media to avoid public mistakes.
Document the 'Why' in your README: Recruiters care more about the architectural decisions you made than the code itself.
Automate your own automation: Use Claude Code to write tests for your scraper to ensure it doesn't break when feed formats change.
content.next_steps: Once your tool is live, add a simple UI dashboard using Next.js to visualize the metrics of your automated posts. This will bridge the gap between a backend script and a full-stack SaaS product.
content.conversion_block: You now have a blueprint to move from 'no ideas' to a functional, high-value asset in your portfolio. Stop waiting for the perfect moment and start building. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
